methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $300M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.0B - $3.0B.

data.narrative:
TurbineOne is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Ian Kalin and Matt Amacker, currently valued at $300M post-money after raising $58M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—across the defense and security sector at equivalent or nearby stages.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $300M last post-money as a base and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, implying a $570M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage private companies, including Dream Security ($3.0B), Harmattan AI ($1.4B), Code Metal ($1.2B), Castelion ($1.0B), and Defense Unicorns ($1.0B), yielding a median valuation of $1.1B with a range of $1.0B to $3.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a range of $484M (low) to $1.3B (high), with a median of $848M. The current $300M valuation sits meaningfully below both method outputs, suggesting upside to the median case. Key uncertainties include the dispersion within the peer set (3.0x between minimum and maximum) and the weighting between stage-progression and peer comparables in a market where defense-tech valuations remain volatile and driven by customer concentration and regulatory dynamics.
